utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run invoked the employment-contract-templates skill in Step 1, which provided legally precise template patterns for critical components including tiered PTO accrual with per-pay-period mechanics, technology monitoring consent with no-privacy-expectation language, and dual-track social media structure. These components are structurally mandated by the skill's templates. The wo_skills run produced a comparable document in terms of length and basic structure, but without the skill's guidance it likely omitted or underspecified the PTO accrual mechanics (J3.3) and technology monitoring consent language (J3.4). Both runs succeeded on the harassment anti-retaliation (J3.1), non-contract disclaimer (J3.2), at-will statement (J3.6), and sick leave separation (J3.7). The wi_skills output scores 7/7 while wo_skills scores 5/7, demonstrating the skill's value in ensuring legally complete policy language for investor-ready documentation.
